title: Mineverse mining action proposal — WRITE contract v1 (TEST GUILD ONLY)
description: The signed action-proposal document the web server POSTs to the bot-side endpoint (MINING_WRITE_ENDPOINT). Contract prose: docs/mining-write-contract.md. The bot executes via disbot/services/mining_workflow.py and MUST audit every web-originated action. Versioning: additive-only within v1 (new enum actions + params sub-schemas edit this file in place); any breaking change ships a new mining_action.v2.schema.json.

contract_version.description: WRITE-contract major version. v1 is exactly the string "1".

action_id.description: Client-generated idempotency key: lowercase UUID v4, unique per intended action. Same action_id + same body = replay of the original response; same action_id + different body = replayed_action rejection.

guild_id.description: Discord guild snowflake as a STRING (IEEE-754 precision — same rationale as the READ contract). v1 executors hard-allowlist TEST guild ids only; anything else is guild_not_allowed.

suid.description: Acting player's superbot user id (Discord user snowflake) as a string. Derived server-side from the verified web session — never client-asserted.

action.description: Proposed op, from the CLOSED v1 enum. Each maps 1:1 to a mining_workflow op; widening the enum is an additive v1 change made in this schema first.

params.description: Per-action parameters; the matching closed sub-schema is selected by the allOf/if-then blocks below. Parameterless actions send {}.

emptyParams.description: No parameters: exactly the empty object.

sellParams.description: sell: which inventory item, how many.

item.description: Item name — an open oracle-owned string, same key space as the READ contract's mining_inventory map.

amountParams.description: vault_deposit / vault_withdraw: how many coins to move.

equipParams.description: equip: which item into which slot. The slot enum is byte-identical to the READ contract's equipment slot set.
